response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack and systems developer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. He lists no Japanese language ability (a hard requirement at native level), his C experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments over a decade ago rather than sustained C-for-UNIX development, and there is no evidence of Linux kernel modification, embedded networking, L2/L3 switch technologies, or security features like MACsec or 802.1X. His strengths lie in web platforms, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang services, which do not transfer to embedded LAN-switch firmware security development.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (18+ years) with strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD)
Some low-level exposure: C/C++ components at National Instruments, low-latency DLang systems, Linux proficiency
Fluent English and demonstrated ability to learn new stacks quickly across domains
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese
No embedded networking, switch, L2/L3, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) experience
No Linux kernel investigation/modification experience; C usage is minimal and dated

key_factors.factor: General engineering seniority and Linux background
